---
title: "Climate-KG Data Analysis Dashboard"
subtitle: "IPCC AR6 Author Demographics and Wikibase Inventory"
---

## Overview

This site presents data analysis and visualizations for the Climate-KG Wikibase project, focusing on IPCC AR6 assessment reports.

### Available Analyses

#### [AR6 Authors Distribution Analysis](research_data/data-vis/ar6-authors-distribution-analysis.html)

Comprehensive demographic analysis of IPCC AR6 authors examining:

- **Global North vs Global South distribution** - Regional representation across reports
- **Gender distribution** - Gender demographics of author teams
- **Intersectional analysis** - Combined gender and regional patterns
- **Chapter-level variations** - Demographic distribution across different chapters

**Key visualizations include:**
- Regional distribution maps and charts
- Gender distribution by region and chapter
- Country-level author contributions
- Report-specific demographic breakdowns

#### [Wikibase Inventory Dashboard](research_data/data-vis/wikibase-inventory-dashboard.html)

Comprehensive inventory and quality analysis of the Climate-KG Wikibase instance:

- Item counts and distribution
- Property usage statistics
- Data quality metrics
- MediaWiki page inventory

---

## About Climate-KG

Climate-KG is a knowledge graph project focused on climate science literature, particularly IPCC assessment reports. The project uses Wikibase to structure and connect climate science data, making it accessible for research and analysis.

### Data Sources

- **Authors**: IPCC AR6 author metadata (demographics, affiliations, contributions)
- **Bibliography**: Climate science literature references
- **Glossary**: Climate science terminology from IPCC reports
- **Acronyms**: Standard climate science abbreviations

### Technology Stack

- **Wikibase**: Knowledge graph database
- **Python**: Data processing and analysis
- **Plotly**: Interactive visualizations
- **Quarto**: Documentation and reporting

---

## Getting Started

All code used to generate these analyses is available in the notebooks. Click on any analysis above to view the interactive reports with collapsible code sections.

**Note**: Code sections are collapsed by default. Click "Show code" to expand and view the Python code used for each analysis.
